generated_from: Live DataForSEO MCP tool metadata available to this Codex session
caveats: The callable tool list is deployment- and module-dependent. Run tools/list in the active MCP client before use.
description_and_exact_tool_declaration is the exact live tool declaration and contains required/optional status, types, accepted values, defaults, limits, and validation notes exposed by the MCP.
underlying_rest_calls are mapped from the official DataForSEO MCP TypeScript source at the recorded revision.

The server appends .ai to underlying REST URLs by default unless DATAFORSEO_FULL_RESPONSE=true or the tool forces a full response. canonical_url_template remains the documented DataForSEO REST endpoint.

MCP output is a CallToolResult containing text. The text is JSON for success or starts with Error: for tool-handled failures; endpoint-specific JSON can be reduced or field-filtered by server configuration.
No REST request body should be built from this catalog when using MCP. The MCP arguments object is the exact request contract; the server maps it into DataForSEO's one-element task array.

dataforseo_labs_google_page_intersection
tools.description_and_exact_tool_declaration:
This endpoint will provide you with the keywords for which specified pages rank within the same SERP. You will get search volume, competition, cost-per-click and impressions data on each intersecting keyword. Along with that, you will get data on SERP elements that specified pages rank for in search results, as well as the estimated traffic volume and cost of ad traffic. Page Intersection endpoint supports organic, paid, local pack and featured snippet results.

Find keywords several webpages rank for:
If you would like to get the keywords several pages rank for, you need to specify webpages only in the pages object. This way, you will receive intersected ranked keywords for the specified URLs.

Find keywords your competitors rank for but you do not:
If you would like to receive all keywords several pages rank for, but particular pages do not, you need to use the exclude_pages array as well. This way you will receive the keywords for which the URLs from the pages object rank for, but the URLs from the exclude_pages array do not

exec tool declaration:
```ts
declare const tools: { mcp__dataforseo__dataforseo_labs_google_page_intersection(args: { exclude_pages?: Array<string>; filters?: Array<Array<string | number | boolean> | "and" | "or" | Array<unknown> | string | number | unknown | unknown>; ignore_synonyms?: boolean; include_clickstream_data?: boolean; intersection_mode?: "union" | "intersect"; item_types?: Array<"organic" | "paid" | "featured_snippet" | "local_pack">; language_code?: string; limit?: number; location_name?: string; offset?: number; order_by?: Array<string>; pages: Array<string>; }): Promise<CallToolResult>; };
```
